body:
### Security intent

This control reduces unnecessary exposure by ensuring that **buckets are not accessible from VMs with open management ports**.

### Network condition reviewed

The assessment reviews buckets accessible from VMs with open management ports in AWS, Google Cloud, Azure / Microsoft Entra, and Alibaba Cloud. It focuses on rules, endpoints, listeners, or policies that make the service reachable in a riskier way than intended.

### Expected posture

Public reachability should be deliberate, documented, and tightly scoped. Administrative and data-plane paths should use private connectivity, strong TLS, explicit source ranges, and provider-native controls rather than broad internet access.
name: Ensure buckets are not accessible from VMs with open management ports
remediationDescription:
## Remediation strategy
1. Close public management ports on VMs.
2. Restrict storage access permissions of VM-attached identities.

## AWS
~~~bash
aws ec2 revoke-security-group-ingress --group-id <sg-id> --protocol tcp --port 22 --cidr 0.0.0.0/0
aws ec2 revoke-security-group-ingress --group-id <sg-id> --protocol tcp --port 3389 --cidr 0.0.0.0/0
~~~

## GCP
~~~bash
gcloud compute firewall-rules update <rule-name> --source-ranges=<trusted-cidr>
~~~

## Azure
~~~bash
az network nsg rule update --resource-group <rg> --nsg-name <nsg> --name <rule> --source-address-prefixes <trusted-cidr>
~~~

### References
- https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/userguide/vpc-security-groups.html
- https://cloud.google.com/firewall/docs/using-firewalls
- https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/network-security-groups-overview
description: Buckets accessible from VMs with open management ports
